steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence contains two independent clauses joined by the transition word "however," which signals a contrast. Standard English requires a period (not a comma) between two independent clauses when a conjunctive adverb like "however" appears at the boundary.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "however. All": The period ends the first independent clause; "however" then opens the contrasting second sentence. This is the correct pattern. ✓
  • Choice B — comma before "however": A comma between two independent clauses creates a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice C — comma after "however": Still leaves the two independent clauses joined without a period, producing a run-on. ✗
  • Choice D — "and": "And" signals addition, not contrast, so it contradicts the meaning signaled by "however." ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
